reasoning: The stock SPGI recently experienced a correction from ~547 in August down to ~470 in mid-October, before bouncing slightly to ~487.35 by October 24. While earnings are scheduled for October 30, adding short-term volatility, there is no strong directional edge heading into the print. The intended spin-off of the S&P Global Mobility division is scheduled to close between April and October 2026 (12 to 18 months from the April 2025 announcement), which is safely outside all three resolution dates, so the stock price will not be artificially lowered by a division spin-off within our timeline. Following the instruction to keep estimates close to 0.5 (as weekly stock moves are largely unpredictable) and to have probabilities for distant dates regress towards 0.5, I assign a slight positive tilt based on broad equity drift and recent bounce momentum, decreasing the probability toward 0.5 for later dates.
